name: scripts
context: Run npm scripts, lifecycle hooks, npx execution, and cross-platform script composition with argument forwarding
info: This sub-skill covers npm script execution: defining and running scripts via `npm run`, built-in shortcuts like `npm test` and `npm start`, pre/post lifecycle hooks that run automatically, the full lifecycle script order for install and publish operations, `npx` and `npm exec` for running packages without global installation, passing arguments to scripts with `--`, cross-platform considerations using `cross-env` and `shx`, and composing complex script pipelines with `npm-run-all` or shell operators. Agents use this skill whenever they need to build, test, lint, or run project-specific commands. See [[node/npm]] for the overall ecosystem and [[node/npm/publish]] for publish-specific lifecycle hooks.
triggers: use npx to run a package
set up pre and post hooks
body:
# npm scripts

Define and run project commands via the `scripts` field in package.json.

## Running Scripts

```bash
# Run a named script
npm run build
npm run lint
npm run dev

# Built-in shortcuts (no "run" needed)
npm test        # runs "test" script
npm start       # runs "start" script
npm stop        # runs "stop" script
npm restart     # runs "stop", "restart", "start" in sequence

# Pass arguments to a script
npm run test -- --watch --coverage
npm run build -- --outdir=dist

# List available scripts
npm run
```

## Defining Scripts in package.json

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsup src/index.ts --format cjs,esm --dts",
    "dev": "tsx watch src/index.ts",
    "test": "vitest",
    "test:watch": "vitest --watch",
    "lint": "eslint src/",
    "lint:fix": "eslint src/ --fix",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "format": "prettier --write \"src/**/*.ts\"",
    "clean": "rm -rf dist node_modules/.cache",
    "prepare": "husky install",
    "prepublishOnly": "npm run build && npm test"
  }
}
```

## Lifecycle Scripts

npm runs certain scripts automatically at specific points:

### Install Lifecycle

1. `preinstall` â€” before `npm install` runs
2. `install` â€” during installation
3. `postinstall` â€” after `npm install` completes
4. `prepare` â€” after install and before pack (runs on `npm install` and `npm publish`)

### Publish Lifecycle

1. `prepublishOnly` â€” before pack and publish (only on `npm publish`)
2. `prepack` â€” before tarball is created
3. `postpack` â€” after tarball is created
4. `publish` â€” after package is published
5. `postpublish` â€” after publish completes

### Version Lifecycle

1. `preversion` â€” before `npm version` bumps
2. `version` â€” after bump, before commit
3. `postversion` â€” after commit and tag

## Pre and Post Hooks

Any script gets automatic `pre` and `post` hooks:

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "prebuild": "npm run clean",
    "build": "tsup src/index.ts",
    "postbuild": "echo 'Build complete'",

    "pretest": "npm run lint",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "posttest": "echo 'Tests passed'"
  }
}
```

When you run `npm run build`, npm executes: `prebuild` -> `build` -> `postbuild`.

## npx â€” Execute Packages

`npx` runs packages without permanently installing them globally.

```bash
# Run a package binary
npx eslint --init
npx create-next-app@latest my-app
npx tsx script.ts

# Run a specific version
npx typescript@5.4 tsc --version

# Skip confirmation prompt
npx --yes degit user/repo my-project

# Equivalent npm exec syntax
npm exec -- eslint --init
npm exec -p typescript -c "tsc --version"
```

**Resolution order**: npx checks `node_modules/.bin` first, then global installs, then downloads temporarily from the registry.

## Script Environment

npm makes several environment variables available inside scripts:

| Variable | Value |
|----------|-------|
| `npm_package_name` | Package name from package.json |
| `npm_package_version` | Package version from package.json |
| `npm_lifecycle_event` | Name of the currently running script |
| `PATH` | Prepended with `node_modules/.bin` |

Because `node_modules/.bin` is on PATH, scripts can reference locally installed binaries directly:

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "lint": "eslint src/"
  }
}
```

This works even though `eslint` is not installed globally â€” npm finds it in `node_modules/.bin/eslint`.

## Cross-Platform Compatibility

Shell commands in npm scripts run in `sh` on Unix and `cmd.exe` on Windows by default. For cross-platform scripts:

```bash
# Use cross-env for environment variables
npm install -D cross-env
```

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build": "cross-env NODE_ENV=production webpack",
    "test": "cross-env NODE_ENV=test vitest"
  }
}
```

```bash
# Use shx for Unix commands on Windows
npm install -D shx
```

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "clean": "shx rm -rf dist",
    "copy": "shx cp -r assets dist/assets"
  }
}
```

## Composing Scripts

Run multiple scripts together:

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build": "npm run clean && npm run compile && npm run bundle",
    "check": "npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test"
  }
}
```

For parallel execution, use `npm-run-all`:

```bash
npm install -D npm-run-all
```

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "lint": "eslint src/",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "check": "run-p lint typecheck test",
    "build:all": "run-s clean build:*"
  }
}
```

- `run-s` â€” run sequentially
- `run-p` â€” run in parallel
- `build:*` â€” glob pattern matches `build:client`, `build:server`, etc.

sections.title: Lifecycle scripts
sections.body: npm runs lifecycle scripts automatically during `install`, `publish`, and `version` commands. Install lifecycle: `preinstall` -> `install` -> `postinstall` -> `prepare`. Publish lifecycle: `prepublishOnly` -> `prepack` -> `postpack` -> `publish` -> `postpublish`. Version lifecycle: `preversion` -> `version` -> `postversion`. The `prepare` script runs after every `npm install` and before `npm publish` â€” commonly used for build steps or git hooks setup (e.g., `husky install`). See [[node/npm/publish]] for publish-specific hooks.

sections.title: Pre and post hooks
sections.body: Any user-defined script gets automatic `pre<name>` and `post<name>` hooks. When you run `npm run build`, npm executes `prebuild` -> `build` -> `postbuild` in sequence. If the pre-hook exits non-zero, the main script does not run. Common patterns: `prebuild` for cleaning, `postbuild` for copying assets, `pretest` for linting, `posttest` for coverage reporting. Pre/post hooks do not work with lifecycle scripts like `prepare` â€” those have their own hook ordering.

sections.title: npx and npm exec
sections.body: `npx` runs packages without permanent global installation. Resolution order: local `node_modules/.bin`, then global installs, then temporary download from registry. Use `npx --yes <pkg>` to skip the confirmation prompt. Use `npx <pkg>@<version>` to run a specific version. The equivalent `npm exec` syntax: `npm exec -- <cmd>` or `npm exec -p <pkg> -c '<cmd>'`. Common uses: `npx create-next-app`, `npx tsx script.ts`, `npx eslint --init`, `npx degit user/repo`. Always specify `@latest` or a version for initializer packages to avoid using stale cached versions.

sections.title: Cross-platform script compatibility
sections.body: npm scripts run in `sh` on Unix and `cmd.exe` on Windows. Shell-specific syntax (pipes, `&&`, `rm -rf`) may break on Windows. Use `cross-env` for setting environment variables portably: `cross-env NODE_ENV=production webpack`. Use `shx` for Unix commands on Windows: `shx rm -rf dist`. Use `npm-run-all` for composing scripts with `run-s` (sequential) and `run-p` (parallel). Alternatively, set `script-shell` in `.npmrc` to force a specific shell. For maximum portability, prefer Node.js-based tools over shell commands in scripts.

related.note: npm must be installed to run scripts defined in package.json
npm install triggers postinstall and prepare lifecycle scripts
npm publish triggers prepublishOnly and prepack lifecycle scripts

scripts.name: Run npm Script
scripts.description: Run an npm script by name with optional argument forwarding and environment variable injection

scriptName.description: Name of the npm script to run (e.g., build, test, lint)

args.description: Arguments to forward to the script after --

env.description: Environment variables to set before running

exitCode.description: Exit code of the script (0 for success)

scriptName.description: Name of the script that was executed

depends.reason: npm CLI required for running scripts defined in package.json
